# workflow-agent-handoff-report-template - Overview

Workflow type: handoff_report
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Make agents report enough evidence that the user can verify the database was actually used.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/handoff-report

When to use:
- Any task that claims to use the local database
- Frontend generation
- Database-backed research
- Agent handoff between tools

Inputs required:
- Task brief
- Queries run
- Chunks returned
- Files changed
- Verification output

AI summary: When an agent uses the local database, final reports must include API/files read, exact queries, chunk ids, how chunks changed the implementation, files changed, verification, and gaps.

# workflow-agent-handoff-report-template - Execution Steps

Workflow type: handoff_report
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Make agents report enough evidence that the user can verify the database was actually used.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/handoff-report

- Record exactly which local files or API endpoints were read.
- List each query and the key record/chunk ids used.
- Explain how retrieved knowledge affected concrete design or implementation choices.
- List changed files and run commands.
- State verification performed and known gaps.

AI summary: When an agent uses the local database, final reports must include API/files read, exact queries, chunk ids, how chunks changed the implementation, files changed, verification, and gaps.

# workflow-agent-handoff-report-template - Prompt Contract

Workflow type: handoff_report
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Make agents report enough evidence that the user can verify the database was actually used.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/handoff-report

- Do not just say 'used the database'; provide query and chunk evidence.
- Keep report concise but auditable.
- Report failures honestly.

AI summary: When an agent uses the local database, final reports must include API/files read, exact queries, chunk ids, how chunks changed the implementation, files changed, verification, and gaps.

# workflow-agent-handoff-report-template - Verification

Workflow type: handoff_report

Evidence: documented / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/handoff-report

- Report contains endpoint/file evidence
- Report contains chunk ids
- Report maps chunks to changes
- Report includes run/test evidence

AI summary: When an agent uses the local database, final reports must include API/files read, exact queries, chunk ids, how chunks changed the implementation, files changed, verification, and gaps.

AI summary: When an agent uses the local database, final reports must include API/files read, exact queries, chunk ids, how chunks changed the implementation, files changed, verification, and gaps.

# workflow-api-first-ui-search - Overview

Workflow type: api_first_retrieval
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Make agents retrieve relevant UI/design knowledge through the local HTTP API before designing or coding.
Evidence: tested / high
Source: http://127.0.0.1:8765/docs

When to use:
- Any frontend or UI design task
- When the user asks to use the local database
- When the task mentions Liquid Glass, animation, dashboard, forms, tables, or visual style

Inputs required:
- User task brief
- Running API at http://127.0.0.1:8765
- Search keywords extracted from the brief

AI summary: Use API-first retrieval for frontend tasks: call /health, derive search queries, call /ui/search, dedupe relevant chunks, design from those chunks, and report queries/chunks used.

# workflow-api-first-ui-search - Execution Steps

Workflow type: api_first_retrieval
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Make agents retrieve relevant UI/design knowledge through the local HTTP API before designing or coding.
Evidence: tested / high
Source: http://127.0.0.1:8765/docs

- Call /health and confirm references/chunks are available.
- Extract 2-5 search queries from the task, including style, page type, components, and motion terms.
- Call /ui/search for each query with limit 5-8.
- Deduplicate chunks by record_id and section.
- Use only relevant chunks as design constraints; ignore unrelated broad matches.
- Proceed to implementation and cite the chunks used in the final report.

AI summary: Use API-first retrieval for frontend tasks: call /health, derive search queries, call /ui/search, dedupe relevant chunks, design from those chunks, and report queries/chunks used.

# workflow-api-first-ui-search - API Calls

Workflow type: api_first_retrieval
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Make agents retrieve relevant UI/design knowledge through the local HTTP API before designing or coding.
Evidence: tested / high
Source: http://127.0.0.1:8765/docs

- GET http://127.0.0.1:8765/health
- GET http://127.0.0.1:8765/ui/search?q={query}&limit=5
- GET http://127.0.0.1:8765/ui/reference/{record_id}

AI summary: Use API-first retrieval for frontend tasks: call /health, derive search queries, call /ui/search, dedupe relevant chunks, design from those chunks, and report queries/chunks used.

# workflow-api-first-ui-search - Prompt Contract

Workflow type: api_first_retrieval
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Make agents retrieve relevant UI/design knowledge through the local HTTP API before designing or coding.
Evidence: tested / high
Source: http://127.0.0.1:8765/docs

- Do not design purely from memory when the local API is available.
- Do not fetch all references by default; use targeted search.
- Report exact queries and key chunks used.

AI summary: Use API-first retrieval for frontend tasks: call /health, derive search queries, call /ui/search, dedupe relevant chunks, design from those chunks, and report queries/chunks used.

# workflow-api-first-ui-search - Verification

Workflow type: api_first_retrieval
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Make agents retrieve relevant UI/design knowledge through the local HTTP API before designing or coding.
Evidence: tested / high
Source: http://127.0.0.1:8765/docs

- /health returns status ok
- Search returns at least one relevant chunk
- Final output maps design decisions to retrieved chunks

AI summary: Use API-first retrieval for frontend tasks: call /health, derive search queries, call /ui/search, dedupe relevant chunks, design from those chunks, and report queries/chunks used.

# workflow-api-first-ui-search - Failure Modes

Workflow type: api_first_retrieval
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents

- API is not running

AI summary: Use API-first retrieval for frontend tasks: call /health, derive search queries, call /ui/search, dedupe relevant chunks, design from those chunks, and report queries/chunks used.

# workflow-api-first-ui-search - Handoff Report

Workflow type: api_first_retrieval
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents

# workflow-knowledge-first-frontend-generation - Overview

Workflow type: knowledge_first_frontend
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, frontend agents
Goal: Generate frontend pages using retrieved design knowledge rather than generic template instincts.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/knowledge-first-frontend

When to use:
- Creating a new frontend page
- Redesigning an existing page
- Testing UI database usefulness
- Building style-specific prototypes

Inputs required:
- Product/page brief
- Target tech stack
- Desired visual style if any
- Required components and states

AI summary: For frontend generation, retrieve page/style/component/motion chunks first, synthesize a design brief, implement with existing stack, include states and accessibility, then report how chunks shaped the page.

# workflow-knowledge-first-frontend-generation - Execution Steps

Workflow type: knowledge_first_frontend
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, frontend agents
Goal: Generate frontend pages using retrieved design knowledge rather than generic template instincts.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/knowledge-first-frontend

- Read the task and identify page type, visual style, components, motion needs, and constraints.
- Search UI knowledge for page type, style, component patterns, and motion patterns.
- Create a short design brief from retrieved chunks: layout, components, states, motion, accessibility, avoid rules.
- Implement the page in the existing tech stack without adding dependencies unless explicitly allowed.
- Include meaningful states: loading, empty, error, success, selected, hover/focus where relevant.
- Run available build/static checks and visually inspect if tooling exists.

AI summary: For frontend generation, retrieve page/style/component/motion chunks first, synthesize a design brief, implement with existing stack, include states and accessibility, then report how chunks shaped the page.

# workflow-knowledge-first-frontend-generation - API Calls

Workflow type: knowledge_first_frontend
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, frontend agents
Goal: Generate frontend pages using retrieved design knowledge rather than generic template instincts.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/knowledge-first-frontend

- GET /ui/search?q={page_type}&limit=5
- GET /ui/search?q={visual_style}&limit=5
- GET /ui/search?q={component_or_motion}&limit=5

AI summary: For frontend generation, retrieve page/style/component/motion chunks first, synthesize a design brief, implement with existing stack, include states and accessibility, then report how chunks shaped the page.

# workflow-knowledge-first-frontend-generation - Prompt Contract

Workflow type: knowledge_first_frontend
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, frontend agents
Goal: Generate frontend pages using retrieved design knowledge rather than generic template instincts.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/knowledge-first-frontend

- Retrieved chunks are constraints, not decoration suggestions.
- If chunks conflict, prioritize direct_spec over screenshot_observed over summary_only.
- If no relevant chunk exists, say what was missing and proceed with a conservative design.

AI summary: For frontend generation, retrieve page/style/component/motion chunks first, synthesize a design brief, implement with existing stack, include states and accessibility, then report how chunks shaped the page.

# workflow-knowledge-first-frontend-generation - Verification

Workflow type: knowledge_first_frontend
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, frontend agents
Goal: Generate frontend pages using retrieved design knowledge rather than generic template instincts.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/knowledge-first-frontend

- Page implements retrieved component/state guidance
- Design is not a generic flat template
- Accessibility/fallback constraints are addressed
- Run/build command succeeds or failure is reported

AI summary: For frontend generation, retrieve page/style/component/motion chunks first, synthesize a design brief, implement with existing stack, include states and accessibility, then report how chunks shaped the page.

# workflow-knowledge-first-frontend-generation - Failure Modes

Workflow type: knowledge_first_frontend
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, frontend agents
Goal: Generate frontend pages using retrieved design knowledge rather than generic template instincts.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/knowledge-first-frontend

- Agent copies the example text but not the design rules
- Agent overuses motion/glass everywhere
- Agent ignores states
- Agent cannot run the project

AI summary: For frontend generation, retrieve page/style/component/motion chunks first, synthesize a design brief, implement with existing stack, include states and accessibility, then report how chunks shaped the page.

# workflow-retrieval-failure-recovery - Overview

Workflow type: failure_recovery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Recover when local knowledge retrieval fails or returns irrelevant results.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/retrieval-failure

When to use:
- /health fails
- /ui/search returns empty
- Search returns broad unrelated chunks
- Agent cannot access local path
- SQLite index missing

Inputs required:
- Error message
- Query attempted
- API status
- Available scripts

AI summary: If local retrieval fails, check API health, simplify or refine queries, rebuild index if missing, fix Windows/Bash paths, and report fallback evidence instead of silently using memory.

# workflow-retrieval-failure-recovery - Execution Steps

Workflow type: failure_recovery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Recover when local knowledge retrieval fails or returns irrelevant results.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/retrieval-failure

- If API is down, start backend_api with uvicorn or fall back to search_ui.py.
- If search is empty, simplify query to one core term, then try synonyms.
- If results are broad, add style/page/component constraints or use a more specific query.
- If SQLite DB is missing, run make_chunks.py and build_sqlite_index.py for the relevant domain.
- If direct script path fails on Windows Bash, use forward slashes or call the HTTP API.
- If no knowledge exists, report the gap and optionally create a new reference record after research.

AI summary: If local retrieval fails, check API health, simplify or refine queries, rebuild index if missing, fix Windows/Bash paths, and report fallback evidence instead of silently using memory.

# workflow-retrieval-failure-recovery - API Calls

Workflow type: failure_recovery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Recover when local knowledge retrieval fails or returns irrelevant results.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/retrieval-failure

- GET /health
- GET /ui/search?q={simplified_query}&limit=5

AI summary: If local retrieval fails, check API health, simplify or refine queries, rebuild index if missing, fix Windows/Bash paths, and report fallback evidence instead of silently using memory.

# workflow-retrieval-failure-recovery - Prompt Contract

Workflow type: failure_recovery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Recover when local knowledge retrieval fails or returns irrelevant results.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/retrieval-failure

- Do not stop after one failed query.
- Retry narrower/broader queries before claiming no data.
- Distinguish no data from API/path failure.

AI summary: If local retrieval fails, check API health, simplify or refine queries, rebuild index if missing, fix Windows/Bash paths, and report fallback evidence instead of silently using memory.

AI summary: If local retrieval fails, check API health, simplify or refine queries, rebuild index if missing, fix Windows/Bash paths, and report fallback evidence instead of silently using memory.

# workflow-windows-path-handling-for-agents - Overview

Workflow type: tool_path_windows
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Bash-based local agents
Goal: Avoid Windows path corruption when agents run commands through Bash or mixed shells.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-observed:2026-04-25/bash-windows-path

When to use:
- Agent runs Bash on Windows
- Command includes E:\DataBase paths
- Python cannot open a file because backslashes disappeared

Inputs required:
- Shell type
- Original command
- Error output
- Target file path

AI summary: On Windows, agents using Bash must avoid raw backslash paths. Use E:/DataBase/... or PowerShell. Prefer the local HTTP API to avoid path handling entirely.

# workflow-windows-path-handling-for-agents - Execution Steps

Workflow type: tool_path_windows
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Bash-based local agents
Goal: Avoid Windows path corruption when agents run commands through Bash or mixed shells.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-observed:2026-04-25/bash-windows-path

- Detect whether the tool is Bash, PowerShell, or cmd.
- In Bash/Git Bash, use forward slashes such as E:/DataBase/scripts/ui_design/search_ui.py or /e/DataBase where supported.
- In PowerShell, native Windows paths with backslashes are safe.
- Quote paths with spaces and avoid unescaped backslashes in Bash.
- If path corruption occurs, rerun the command with forward slashes.

AI summary: On Windows, agents using Bash must avoid raw backslash paths. Use E:/DataBase/... or PowerShell. Prefer the local HTTP API to avoid path handling entirely.

# workflow-windows-path-handling-for-agents - Prompt Contract

Workflow type: tool_path_windows
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Bash-based local agents
Goal: Avoid Windows path corruption when agents run commands through Bash or mixed shells.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-observed:2026-04-25/bash-windows-path

- Do not use raw Windows backslash paths in Bash commands.
- Prefer local API calls over direct script paths when the API is running.

AI summary: On Windows, agents using Bash must avoid raw backslash paths. Use E:/DataBase/... or PowerShell. Prefer the local HTTP API to avoid path handling entirely.

# workflow-windows-path-handling-for-agents - Verification

Workflow type: tool_path_windows
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Bash-based local agents
Goal: Avoid Windows path corruption when agents run commands through Bash or mixed shells.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-observed:2026-04-25/bash-windows-path

- Command resolves the intended file
- No duplicated path fragments such as DataBaseDataBasescripts
- Python script starts or API responds

AI summary: On Windows, agents using Bash must avoid raw backslash paths. Use E:/DataBase/... or PowerShell. Prefer the local HTTP API to avoid path handling entirely.

# workflow-windows-path-handling-for-agents - Failure Modes

Workflow type: tool_path_windows
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Bash-based local agents
Goal: Avoid Windows path corruption when agents run commands through Bash or mixed shells.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-observed:2026-04-25/bash-windows-path

- Backslashes are consumed as escape characters
- Drive path is treated as relative
- Working directory duplicates path segments

AI summary: On Windows, agents using Bash must avoid raw backslash paths. Use E:/DataBase/... or PowerShell. Prefer the local HTTP API to avoid path handling entirely.
